content.subject: Add field-offset consts + size assert to context.rs
content.description: Add REGS_X_OFFSET, REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, REGS_ELR_OFFSET, REGS_SPSR_OFFSET and a size assert (272 bytes) so trap.S can use matching .equ values.

content.subject: Add Limine KernelAddressRequest + kernel_va_to_pa helper
content.description: Wire the LIMINE_KERNEL_ADDRESS_REQUEST into limine.rs, expose kernel_va_to_pa(va: u64) -> Option<u64> for translating kernel-image VAs to PAs (needed to derive PA for static page-table storage and the user stub blob).

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/mmu.rs (PTE bits, PageTable, map/activate/flush helpers)
content.description: PTE bit constants, 4KB-aligned PageTable type, map_user_page_4k walker, activate_user_ttbr0 (TCR_EL1 read-modify-write masking only T0SZ/IRGN0/ORGN0/SH0/TG0/EPD0), MAIR_EL1 sanity-assert, flush_icache_range.

content.subject: Create user_stub.S (EL0 instruction blob in rodata)
content.description: PC-relative-free instructions in .rodata.user_stub bracketed by _user_stub_start/_user_stub_end: load IPC args into x0/x1/x2, svc #0, b _user_stub_start.

content.subject: Create trap.S (SVC entry stub + el1_return_to_user)
content.description: el0_64_sync_entry: reads TPIDR_EL1 to find frame, decodes ESR_EL1 EC, saves x0-x30 + SP_EL0/ELR_EL1/SPSR_EL1 directly into &mut ArchRegisterFrame, calls do_ipc, tail-calls el1_return_to_user. el1_return_to_user reads TPIDR_EL1, restores frame, erets. Mirror REGS_* offsets via .equ with assertion canary.

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/userland.rs (bootstrap + PT/page storage)
content.description: Static UnsafeCell-wrapped PT arena (L0/L1/L2/L3-code/L3-stack) + code page + stack page, USER_CODE_VA/USER_STACK_VA consts, userland_bootstrap() that wires L0..L3, activates TTBR0, copies stub bytes into code page with i-cache flush, populates the ProcNr 11 slot, sets CURRENT_PROC_NR.

content.subject: Create proc/sched.rs (CURRENT_PROC_NR + switch_to_user)
content.description: AtomicI32 CURRENT_PROC_NR; current_proc_mut() helper; switch_to_user(&mut Proc) -> ! that parks &mut p.regs in TPIDR_EL1 and jumps to el1_return_to_user (extern from trap.S).

content.subject: Create ipc/mod.rs (do_ipc stub)
content.description: extern "C" fn do_ipc(frame: &mut ArchRegisterFrame): prints do_ipc[n]: call_nr=... src_dst=... msg=... over UART, places OK (0) into frame.x[0]. Module-level CALL_COUNT AtomicU32.

content.subject: Specialize vector slot 8 in vectors.S
content.description: Replace EXC_ENTRY el0_64_sync, 8 with a hand-coded slot that just does b el0_64_sync_entry. Keep all 15 other vectors on the panic macro.

content.subject: Wire new .S files into build.rs and new mods into arch/proc/main
content.description: Append trap.S and user_stub.S to build.rs sources; add `pub mod mmu; pub mod userland;` to arch/aarch64/mod.rs; add `pub mod sched;` to proc/mod.rs; add `mod ipc;` to main.rs.

content.subject: Update kmain to launch the EL0 stub task
content.description: After proc::dump_tables, print "entering EL0 stub task..."; call arch::userland::userland_bootstrap(); call proc::sched::switch_to_user(current_proc_mut().unwrap()). Remove trailing arch::halt() (only reached on early failure).

content.subject: Build kernel and boot in QEMU to verify do_ipc round-trips
content.description: timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release; confirm "entering EL0 stub task..." followed by repeating "do_ipc[n]: call_nr=3 src_dst=-2 msg=0x0..." lines until timeout.

content.subject: Update docs/plan.md to mark slice 2.3 ready / 2.4 next
content.description: Flip slice 2.3 to "◀ ready (branch feature/phase-2-3-el0-svc-entry, pending merge)" and slide "◀ next" to slice 2.4.

content.subject: Stage all changes and surface for review (no commit in auto mode)
content.description: git add the changed files; show git status + summary; do NOT git commit (per user CLAUDE.md auto-mode rule).

lastPrompt: work on slice 2.3 of phase 2 in @docs/plan.md

content.subject: Add field-offset consts + size assert to context.rs
content.description: Add REGS_X_OFFSET, REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, REGS_ELR_OFFSET, REGS_SPSR_OFFSET and a size assert (272 bytes) so trap.S can use matching .equ values.

content.subject: Add Limine KernelAddressRequest + kernel_va_to_pa helper
content.description: Wire the LIMINE_KERNEL_ADDRESS_REQUEST into limine.rs, expose kernel_va_to_pa(va: u64) -> Option<u64> for translating kernel-image VAs to PAs (needed to derive PA for static page-table storage and the user stub blob).

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/mmu.rs (PTE bits, PageTable, map/activate/flush helpers)
content.description: PTE bit constants, 4KB-aligned PageTable type, map_user_page_4k walker, activate_user_ttbr0 (TCR_EL1 read-modify-write masking only T0SZ/IRGN0/ORGN0/SH0/TG0/EPD0), MAIR_EL1 sanity-assert, flush_icache_range.

content.subject: Create user_stub.S (EL0 instruction blob in rodata)
content.description: PC-relative-free instructions in .rodata.user_stub bracketed by _user_stub_start/_user_stub_end: load IPC args into x0/x1/x2, svc #0, b _user_stub_start.

content.subject: Create trap.S (SVC entry stub + el1_return_to_user)
content.description: el0_64_sync_entry: reads TPIDR_EL1 to find frame, decodes ESR_EL1 EC, saves x0-x30 + SP_EL0/ELR_EL1/SPSR_EL1 directly into &mut ArchRegisterFrame, calls do_ipc, tail-calls el1_return_to_user. el1_return_to_user reads TPIDR_EL1, restores frame, erets. Mirror REGS_* offsets via .equ with assertion canary.

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/userland.rs (bootstrap + PT/page storage)
content.description: Static UnsafeCell-wrapped PT arena (L0/L1/L2/L3-code/L3-stack) + code page + stack page, USER_CODE_VA/USER_STACK_VA consts, userland_bootstrap() that wires L0..L3, activates TTBR0, copies stub bytes into code page with i-cache flush, populates the ProcNr 11 slot, sets CURRENT_PROC_NR.

content.subject: Create proc/sched.rs (CURRENT_PROC_NR + switch_to_user)
content.description: AtomicI32 CURRENT_PROC_NR; current_proc_mut() helper; switch_to_user(&mut Proc) -> ! that parks &mut p.regs in TPIDR_EL1 and jumps to el1_return_to_user (extern from trap.S).

content.subject: Create ipc/mod.rs (do_ipc stub)
content.description: extern "C" fn do_ipc(frame: &mut ArchRegisterFrame): prints do_ipc[n]: call_nr=... src_dst=... msg=... over UART, places OK (0) into frame.x[0]. Module-level CALL_COUNT AtomicU32.

content.subject: Specialize vector slot 8 in vectors.S
content.description: Replace EXC_ENTRY el0_64_sync, 8 with a hand-coded slot that just does b el0_64_sync_entry. Keep all 15 other vectors on the panic macro.

content.subject: Wire new .S files into build.rs and new mods into arch/proc/main
content.description: Append trap.S and user_stub.S to build.rs sources; add `pub mod mmu; pub mod userland;` to arch/aarch64/mod.rs; add `pub mod sched;` to proc/mod.rs; add `mod ipc;` to main.rs.

content.subject: Update kmain to launch the EL0 stub task
content.description: After proc::dump_tables, print "entering EL0 stub task..."; call arch::userland::userland_bootstrap(); call proc::sched::switch_to_user(current_proc_mut().unwrap()). Remove trailing arch::halt() (only reached on early failure).

content.subject: Build kernel and boot in QEMU to verify do_ipc round-trips
content.description: timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release; confirm "entering EL0 stub task..." followed by repeating "do_ipc[n]: call_nr=3 src_dst=-2 msg=0x0..." lines until timeout.

content.text: Let me try removing my TCR modification entirely — Limine has already set TG0/T0SZ/IRGN0/ORGN0/SH0 correctly per the diag. The only thing I need is to make sure EPD0 is cleared.

content.content: The file /Users/kevinbarnard/src/minix-4/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/mmu.rs has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: -/// Install `root_pa` as TTBR0_EL1 and program the TTBR0-side fields of
-/// TCR_EL1 (T0SZ=16 → 48-bit VA, IRGN0=ORGN0=WBWA, SH0=Inner, TG0=4 KiB,
+/// Install `root_pa` as TTBR0_EL1 and ensure TTBR0 walks are enabled.
+/// Limine has already programmed TCR_EL1 with T0SZ=16, TG0=4 KiB,
+/// IRGN0=ORGN0=WBWA, SH0=Inner (verified by the diagnostic print in kmain),
+/// so we only need to clear EPD0 if set. We deliberately read-modify-write
+/// just bit 7 — touching any other TCR field risks perturbing TTBR1, which
+/// Limine owns and whose translations the kernel is actively using.
/// SAFETY: must be called with interrupts masked. `root_pa` must point at a
-    // Read-modify-write TCR_EL1, clearing only the `*0` field-set bits.
// SAFETY: TTBR0_EL1, TCR_EL1, and TLB-maintenance ops are EL1-only and
-    // touch no normal memory. We mask off only TTBR0 fields, leaving every
+    // touch no normal memory. Bit 7 (EPD0) is the only TCR field we
+    // perturb; the rest stays as Limine left it.
asm!("mrs {0}, tcr_el1", out(reg) tcr, options(nomem, nostack, preserves_flags));

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * EL0 stub task for slice 2.3.
 *
 * Lives in `.rodata.user_stub` inside the kernel image; `userland_bootstrap`
 * copies these bytes into the user code page (mapped EL0 RX at
 * USER_CODE_VA) before erets. Every instruction here is PC-relative-free, so
 * the same encoding works at any VA — no relocations required.
 *
 * What it does:
 *   - x0 = 0x7FFE  (boot endpoint for SYSTEM, i.e. make_endpoint(0, -2))
 *   - x1 = 3        (SENDREC IPC primitive)
 *   - x2 = 0        (no message pointer; the slice-2.3 do_ipc stub ignores it)
 *   - svc #0        (trap into the kernel SVC handler)
 *   - b _user_stub_start  (loop forever to keep generating do_ipc calls)
 */

    .section .rodata.user_stub, "a", %progbits
    .balign 4
    .global _user_stub_start
    .type _user_stub_start, %object
_user_stub_start:
    mov     x0, #0x7FFE
    mov     x1, #3
    mov     x2, xzr
    svc     #0
    b       _user_stub_start
    .global _user_stub_end
_user_stub_end:
    .size _user_stub_start, _user_stub_end - _user_stub_start

content.subject: Add field-offset consts + size assert to context.rs
content.description: Add REGS_X_OFFSET, REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, REGS_ELR_OFFSET, REGS_SPSR_OFFSET and a size assert (272 bytes) so trap.S can use matching .equ values.

content.subject: Add Limine KernelAddressRequest + kernel_va_to_pa helper
content.description: Wire the LIMINE_KERNEL_ADDRESS_REQUEST into limine.rs, expose kernel_va_to_pa(va: u64) -> Option<u64> for translating kernel-image VAs to PAs (needed to derive PA for static page-table storage and the user stub blob).

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/mmu.rs (PTE bits, PageTable, map/activate/flush helpers)
content.description: PTE bit constants, 4KB-aligned PageTable type, map_user_page_4k walker, activate_user_ttbr0 (TCR_EL1 read-modify-write masking only T0SZ/IRGN0/ORGN0/SH0/TG0/EPD0), MAIR_EL1 sanity-assert, flush_icache_range.

content.subject: Create user_stub.S (EL0 instruction blob in rodata)
content.description: PC-relative-free instructions in .rodata.user_stub bracketed by _user_stub_start/_user_stub_end: load IPC args into x0/x1/x2, svc #0, b _user_stub_start.

content.subject: Create trap.S (SVC entry stub + el1_return_to_user)
content.description: el0_64_sync_entry: reads TPIDR_EL1 to find frame, decodes ESR_EL1 EC, saves x0-x30 + SP_EL0/ELR_EL1/SPSR_EL1 directly into &mut ArchRegisterFrame, calls do_ipc, tail-calls el1_return_to_user. el1_return_to_user reads TPIDR_EL1, restores frame, erets. Mirror REGS_* offsets via .equ with assertion canary.

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/userland.rs (bootstrap + PT/page storage)
content.description: Static UnsafeCell-wrapped PT arena (L0/L1/L2/L3-code/L3-stack) + code page + stack page, USER_CODE_VA/USER_STACK_VA consts, userland_bootstrap() that wires L0..L3, activates TTBR0, copies stub bytes into code page with i-cache flush, populates the ProcNr 11 slot, sets CURRENT_PROC_NR.

content.subject: Create proc/sched.rs (CURRENT_PROC_NR + switch_to_user)
content.description: AtomicI32 CURRENT_PROC_NR; current_proc_mut() helper; switch_to_user(&mut Proc) -> ! that parks &mut p.regs in TPIDR_EL1 and jumps to el1_return_to_user (extern from trap.S).

content.subject: Create ipc/mod.rs (do_ipc stub)
content.description: extern "C" fn do_ipc(frame: &mut ArchRegisterFrame): prints do_ipc[n]: call_nr=... src_dst=... msg=... over UART, places OK (0) into frame.x[0]. Module-level CALL_COUNT AtomicU32.

content.subject: Specialize vector slot 8 in vectors.S
content.description: Replace EXC_ENTRY el0_64_sync, 8 with a hand-coded slot that just does b el0_64_sync_entry. Keep all 15 other vectors on the panic macro.

content.subject: Wire new .S files into build.rs and new mods into arch/proc/main
content.description: Append trap.S and user_stub.S to build.rs sources; add `pub mod mmu; pub mod userland;` to arch/aarch64/mod.rs; add `pub mod sched;` to proc/mod.rs; add `mod ipc;` to main.rs.

content.subject: Update kmain to launch the EL0 stub task
content.description: After proc::dump_tables, print "entering EL0 stub task..."; call arch::userland::userland_bootstrap(); call proc::sched::switch_to_user(current_proc_mut().unwrap()). Remove trailing arch::halt() (only reached on early failure).

content.subject: Build kernel and boot in QEMU to verify do_ipc round-trips
content.description: timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release; confirm "entering EL0 stub task..." followed by repeating "do_ipc[n]: call_nr=3 src_dst=-2 msg=0x0..." lines until timeout.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * MINIX 4 aarch64 EL0 → EL1 trap entry + eret stub.
 *
 * Used for:
 *   - EL0 SVC traps (vector slot 8). `vectors.S` branches here unconditionally
 *     for that slot; this stub saves user state into CURRENT_PROC->regs,
 *     decodes ESR_EL1, and dispatches either `do_ipc` (EC = SVC64) or the
 *     unexpected-sync panic helper.
 *   - Returning to EL0. `switch_to_user` and the SVC tail both branch to
 *     `el1_return_to_user`, which restores `tpidr_el1`-pointed regs and erets.
 *
 * Calling convention:
 *   - TPIDR_EL1 always holds `&mut CURRENT_PROC->regs` while we are running
 *     a user context. `switch_to_user` sets it before the first eret; we
 *     never clear it.
 *   - Field offsets below must match `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`.
 *     The .equ block is mirrored from the Rust REGS_*_OFFSET constants; the
 *     `const _: () = assert!(size_of::<ArchRegisterFrame> == 272)` canary in
 *     context.rs catches drift in the trailing fields.
 */

    .equ REGS_X_OFFSET,      0
    .equ REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, 248
    .equ REGS_ELR_OFFSET,    256
    .equ REGS_SPSR_OFFSET,   264

    .equ ESR_EC_SVC64,       0x15

    .section .text.trap, "ax", %progbits

/*
 * el0_64_sync_entry — entry stub for vector slot 8 (EL0 AArch64 sync).
 *
 * On entry:
 *   - EL1h, SP = SP_EL1 (kernel stack) is live and usable.
 *   - All EL0 user regs x0..x30 hold caller values, unsaved.
 *   - TPIDR_EL1 = &mut CURRENT_PROC->regs.
 *   - ESR/ELR/SPSR_EL1 describe the trap.
 *
 * We spill x29/x30 to the kernel stack to free two scratch registers
 * (x29 will be the frame pointer; x30 is reloaded from the stack into a
 * scratch reg before being written into the frame). All other user regs
 * are saved directly into the frame via stp/str, then SP_EL0 / ELR_EL1 /
 * SPSR_EL1 follow. EC is decoded from ESR_EL1; SVC64 → do_ipc, anything
 * else → el0_sync_unexpected.
 */
    .global el0_64_sync_entry
    .type el0_64_sync_entry, %function
el0_64_sync_entry:
    // Free x29/x30 for use as scratch (frame pointer + temporary).
    stp     x29, x30, [sp, #-16]!

    // x29 = &frame.
    mrs     x29, tpidr_el1

    // Save x0..x28 into the frame. x29 (frame ptr) cannot be its own base
    // and destination simultaneously, so we save the user's x29/x30 below.
    stp     x0,  x1,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x00]
    stp     x2,  x3,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x10]
    stp     x4,  x5,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x20]
    stp     x6,  x7,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x30]
    stp     x8,  x9,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x40]
    stp     x10, x11, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x50]
    stp     x12, x13, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x60]
    stp     x14, x15, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x70]
    stp     x16, x17, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x80]
    stp     x18, x19, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x90]
    stp     x20, x21, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xA0]
    stp     x22, x23, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xB0]
    stp     x24, x25, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xC0]
    stp     x26, x27, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xD0]
    str     x28,      [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE0]

    // Pop the user's x29 and x30 off the kernel stack into scratch regs
    // and store them into the frame.
    ldp     x16, x17, [sp], #16
    stp     x16, x17, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE8]

    // Save SP_EL0, ELR_EL1, SPSR_EL1.
    mrs     x9,  sp_el0
    mrs     x10, elr_el1
    mrs     x11, spsr_el1
    stp     x9,  x10, [x29, #REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET]
    str     x11,      [x29, #REGS_SPSR_OFFSET]

    // Decode ESR_EL1 EC and dispatch.
    mrs     x12, esr_el1
    lsr     x13, x12, #26
    cmp     x13, #ESR_EC_SVC64
    b.ne    1f

    // SVC64 — call do_ipc(&mut frame).
    mov     x0, x29
    bl      do_ipc
    b       el1_return_to_user

1:  // Non-SVC EL0 sync exception — diagnose and panic.
    // Args: el0_sync_unexpected(esr, elr, far).
    mov     x0, x12              // esr_el1
    mrs     x1, elr_el1
    mrs     x2, far_el1
    bl      el0_sync_unexpected
    // el0_sync_unexpected is -> !; if it ever returns, halt.
2:  wfe
    b       2b
    .size el0_64_sync_entry, . - el0_64_sync_entry

/*
 * el1_return_to_user — restore TPIDR_EL1-pointed frame and eret.
 *
 * Used by:
 *   - proc::sched::switch_to_user (first entry into EL0).
 *   - SVC tail (after do_ipc returns).
 *
 * Both paths leave TPIDR_EL1 pointing at the frame. x9 stays the base
 * throughout; x8 and x9 are restored last (otherwise loading into x9
 * would clobber the base).
 */
    .global el1_return_to_user
    .type el1_return_to_user, %function
el1_return_to_user:
    mrs     x9, tpidr_el1

    // System registers first.
    ldp     x10, x11, [x9, #REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET]   // sp_el0, elr_el1
    ldr     x12,      [x9, #REGS_SPSR_OFFSET]
    msr     sp_el0,   x10
    msr     elr_el1,  x11
    msr     spsr_el1, x12

    // GPRs. Skip x8/x9 here — they'd clobber the base mid-restore.
    ldp     x0,  x1,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x00]
    ldp     x2,  x3,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x10]
    ldp     x4,  x5,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x20]
    ldp     x6,  x7,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x30]
    ldp     x10, x11, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x50]
    ldp     x12, x13, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x60]
    ldp     x14, x15, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x70]
    ldp     x16, x17, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x80]
    ldp     x18, x19, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x90]
    ldp     x20, x21, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xA0]
    ldp     x22, x23, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xB0]
    ldp     x24, x25, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xC0]
    ldp     x26, x27, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xD0]
    ldp     x28, x29, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE0]
    ldr     x30,      [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xF0]

    // Finally x8 and x9 — load x9 last so the base survives until needed.
    ldr     x8,       [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x40]
    ldr     x9,       [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x48]

    eret
    .size el1_return_to_user, . - el1_return_to_user

content.text: Found it. Limine left us at EL1t with SPSel=0 (kernel uses SP_EL0), so SP_EL1 was never set. On SVC trap entry, the CPU forces SPSel=1 and tries to push onto an uninitialized SP_EL1. Fix: explicitly set SP_EL1 = current SP at kmain entry.

lines: +    // Limine hands the kernel control at EL1t (SPSel=0), so all our boot-
+    // time stack operations run on SP_EL0. That's fine for kmain itself,
+    // but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous exception the CPU forces
+    // SPSel=1 — meaning the vector handler runs on SP_EL1, which Limine
+    // never initializes. Copy the current SP into SP_EL1 so the boot
+    // stack is reachable from both stack pointers; this is safe because
+    // the kernel does no work concurrently with a trap-from-EL0 handler
+    // (control sits in EL0 until the next trap fires).
+    // Slice 2.4 (timer + preemption) will revisit this when interrupts
+    // start firing from EL1 itself — at that point we need a dedicated
+    // trap stack so a nested IRQ doesn't clobber the kernel's working
+    // SAFETY: writing SP_EL1 from EL1t does not change the active SP
+    // (active SP is SP_EL0 because SPSel=0); the boot stack is reachable
+    // through both registers afterward. No memory effects.

content.subject: Add field-offset consts + size assert to context.rs
content.description: Add REGS_X_OFFSET, REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, REGS_ELR_OFFSET, REGS_SPSR_OFFSET and a size assert (272 bytes) so trap.S can use matching .equ values.

content.subject: Add Limine KernelAddressRequest + kernel_va_to_pa helper
content.description: Wire the LIMINE_KERNEL_ADDRESS_REQUEST into limine.rs, expose kernel_va_to_pa(va: u64) -> Option<u64> for translating kernel-image VAs to PAs (needed to derive PA for static page-table storage and the user stub blob).

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/mmu.rs (PTE bits, PageTable, map/activate/flush helpers)
content.description: PTE bit constants, 4KB-aligned PageTable type, map_user_page_4k walker, activate_user_ttbr0 (TCR_EL1 read-modify-write masking only T0SZ/IRGN0/ORGN0/SH0/TG0/EPD0), MAIR_EL1 sanity-assert, flush_icache_range.

content.subject: Create user_stub.S (EL0 instruction blob in rodata)
content.description: PC-relative-free instructions in .rodata.user_stub bracketed by _user_stub_start/_user_stub_end: load IPC args into x0/x1/x2, svc #0, b _user_stub_start.

content.subject: Create trap.S (SVC entry stub + el1_return_to_user)
content.description: el0_64_sync_entry: reads TPIDR_EL1 to find frame, decodes ESR_EL1 EC, saves x0-x30 + SP_EL0/ELR_EL1/SPSR_EL1 directly into &mut ArchRegisterFrame, calls do_ipc, tail-calls el1_return_to_user. el1_return_to_user reads TPIDR_EL1, restores frame, erets. Mirror REGS_* offsets via .equ with assertion canary.

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/userland.rs (bootstrap + PT/page storage)
content.description: Static UnsafeCell-wrapped PT arena (L0/L1/L2/L3-code/L3-stack) + code page + stack page, USER_CODE_VA/USER_STACK_VA consts, userland_bootstrap() that wires L0..L3, activates TTBR0, copies stub bytes into code page with i-cache flush, populates the ProcNr 11 slot, sets CURRENT_PROC_NR.

content.subject: Create proc/sched.rs (CURRENT_PROC_NR + switch_to_user)
content.description: AtomicI32 CURRENT_PROC_NR; current_proc_mut() helper; switch_to_user(&mut Proc) -> ! that parks &mut p.regs in TPIDR_EL1 and jumps to el1_return_to_user (extern from trap.S).

content.subject: Create ipc/mod.rs (do_ipc stub)
content.description: extern "C" fn do_ipc(frame: &mut ArchRegisterFrame): prints do_ipc[n]: call_nr=... src_dst=... msg=... over UART, places OK (0) into frame.x[0]. Module-level CALL_COUNT AtomicU32.

content.subject: Specialize vector slot 8 in vectors.S
content.description: Replace EXC_ENTRY el0_64_sync, 8 with a hand-coded slot that just does b el0_64_sync_entry. Keep all 15 other vectors on the panic macro.

content.subject: Wire new .S files into build.rs and new mods into arch/proc/main
content.description: Append trap.S and user_stub.S to build.rs sources; add `pub mod mmu; pub mod userland;` to arch/aarch64/mod.rs; add `pub mod sched;` to proc/mod.rs; add `mod ipc;` to main.rs.

content.subject: Update kmain to launch the EL0 stub task
content.description: After proc::dump_tables, print "entering EL0 stub task..."; call arch::userland::userland_bootstrap(); call proc::sched::switch_to_user(current_proc_mut().unwrap()). Remove trailing arch::halt() (only reached on early failure).

content.subject: Build kernel and boot in QEMU to verify do_ipc round-trips
content.description: timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release; confirm "entering EL0 stub task..." followed by repeating "do_ipc[n]: call_nr=3 src_dst=-2 msg=0x0..." lines until timeout.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * MINIX 4 aarch64 EL0 → EL1 trap entry + eret stub.
 *
 * Used for:
 *   - EL0 SVC traps (vector slot 8). `vectors.S` branches here unconditionally
 *     for that slot; this stub saves user state into CURRENT_PROC->regs,
 *     decodes ESR_EL1, and dispatches either `do_ipc` (EC = SVC64) or the
 *     unexpected-sync panic helper.
 *   - Returning to EL0. `switch_to_user` and the SVC tail both branch to
 *     `el1_return_to_user`, which restores `tpidr_el1`-pointed regs and erets.
 *
 * Calling convention:
 *   - TPIDR_EL1 always holds `&mut CURRENT_PROC->regs` while we are running
 *     a user context. `switch_to_user` sets it before the first eret; we
 *     never clear it.
 *   - Field offsets below must match `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/context.rs`.
 *     The .equ block is mirrored from the Rust REGS_*_OFFSET constants; the
 *     `const _: () = assert!(size_of::<ArchRegisterFrame> == 272)` canary in
 *     context.rs catches drift in the trailing fields.
 */

    .equ REGS_X_OFFSET,      0
    .equ REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, 248
    .equ REGS_ELR_OFFSET,    256
    .equ REGS_SPSR_OFFSET,   264

    .equ ESR_EC_SVC64,       0x15

    .section .text.trap, "ax", %progbits

/*
 * el0_64_sync_entry — entry stub for vector slot 8 (EL0 AArch64 sync).
 *
 * On entry:
 *   - EL1h, SP = SP_EL1 (kernel stack) is live and usable.
 *   - All EL0 user regs x0..x30 hold caller values, unsaved.
 *   - TPIDR_EL1 = &mut CURRENT_PROC->regs.
 *   - ESR/ELR/SPSR_EL1 describe the trap.
 *
 * We spill x29/x30 to the kernel stack to free two scratch registers
 * (x29 will be the frame pointer; x30 is reloaded from the stack into a
 * scratch reg before being written into the frame). All other user regs
 * are saved directly into the frame via stp/str, then SP_EL0 / ELR_EL1 /
 * SPSR_EL1 follow. EC is decoded from ESR_EL1; SVC64 → do_ipc, anything
 * else → el0_sync_unexpected.
 */
    .global el0_64_sync_entry
    .type el0_64_sync_entry, %function
el0_64_sync_entry:
    // DIAG slice 2.3: capture SP and print a marker before doing anything
    // that could fault. Saves x0..x2 + x29/x30 + LR around the call so we
    // can still proceed into the regular handler below.
    stp     x29, x30, [sp, #-16]!
    stp     x0,  x1,  [sp, #-16]!
    stp     x2,  x3,  [sp, #-16]!
    mov     x0, sp
    bl      diag_svc_marker
    ldp     x2,  x3,  [sp], #16
    ldp     x0,  x1,  [sp], #16
    ldp     x29, x30, [sp], #16

    // Free x29/x30 for use as scratch (frame pointer + temporary).
    stp     x29, x30, [sp, #-16]!

    // x29 = &frame.
    mrs     x29, tpidr_el1

    // Save x0..x28 into the frame. x29 (frame ptr) cannot be its own base
    // and destination simultaneously, so we save the user's x29/x30 below.
    stp     x0,  x1,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x00]
    stp     x2,  x3,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x10]
    stp     x4,  x5,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x20]
    stp     x6,  x7,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x30]
    stp     x8,  x9,  [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x40]
    stp     x10, x11, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x50]
    stp     x12, x13, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x60]
    stp     x14, x15, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x70]
    stp     x16, x17, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x80]
    stp     x18, x19, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x90]
    stp     x20, x21, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xA0]
    stp     x22, x23, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xB0]
    stp     x24, x25, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xC0]
    stp     x26, x27, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xD0]
    str     x28,      [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE0]

    // Pop the user's x29 and x30 off the kernel stack into scratch regs
    // and store them into the frame.
    ldp     x16, x17, [sp], #16
    stp     x16, x17, [x29, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE8]

    // Save SP_EL0, ELR_EL1, SPSR_EL1.
    mrs     x9,  sp_el0
    mrs     x10, elr_el1
    mrs     x11, spsr_el1
    stp     x9,  x10, [x29, #REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET]
    str     x11,      [x29, #REGS_SPSR_OFFSET]

    // Decode ESR_EL1 EC and dispatch.
    mrs     x12, esr_el1
    lsr     x13, x12, #26
    cmp     x13, #ESR_EC_SVC64
    b.ne    1f

    // SVC64 — call do_ipc(&mut frame).
    mov     x0, x29
    bl      do_ipc
    b       el1_return_to_user

1:  // Non-SVC EL0 sync exception — diagnose and panic.
    // Args: el0_sync_unexpected(esr, elr, far).
    mov     x0, x12              // esr_el1
    mrs     x1, elr_el1
    mrs     x2, far_el1
    bl      el0_sync_unexpected
    // el0_sync_unexpected is -> !; if it ever returns, halt.
2:  wfe
    b       2b
    .size el0_64_sync_entry, . - el0_64_sync_entry

/*
 * el1_return_to_user — restore TPIDR_EL1-pointed frame and eret.
 *
 * Used by:
 *   - proc::sched::switch_to_user (first entry into EL0).
 *   - SVC tail (after do_ipc returns).
 *
 * Both paths leave TPIDR_EL1 pointing at the frame. x9 stays the base
 * throughout; x8 and x9 are restored last (otherwise loading into x9
 * would clobber the base).
 */
    .global el1_return_to_user
    .type el1_return_to_user, %function
el1_return_to_user:
    mrs     x9, tpidr_el1

    // System registers first.
    ldp     x10, x11, [x9, #REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET]   // sp_el0, elr_el1
    ldr     x12,      [x9, #REGS_SPSR_OFFSET]
    msr     sp_el0,   x10
    msr     elr_el1,  x11
    msr     spsr_el1, x12

    // GPRs. Skip x8/x9 here — they'd clobber the base mid-restore.
    ldp     x0,  x1,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x00]
    ldp     x2,  x3,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x10]
    ldp     x4,  x5,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x20]
    ldp     x6,  x7,  [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x30]
    ldp     x10, x11, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x50]
    ldp     x12, x13, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x60]
    ldp     x14, x15, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x70]
    ldp     x16, x17, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x80]
    ldp     x18, x19, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x90]
    ldp     x20, x21, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xA0]
    ldp     x22, x23, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xB0]
    ldp     x24, x25, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xC0]
    ldp     x26, x27, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xD0]
    ldp     x28, x29, [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xE0]
    ldr     x30,      [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0xF0]

    // Finally x8 and x9 — load x9 last so the base survives until needed.
    ldr     x8,       [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x40]
    ldr     x9,       [x9, #REGS_X_OFFSET + 0x48]

    eret
    .size el1_return_to_user, . - el1_return_to_user

content.subject: Add field-offset consts + size assert to context.rs
content.description: Add REGS_X_OFFSET, REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, REGS_ELR_OFFSET, REGS_SPSR_OFFSET and a size assert (272 bytes) so trap.S can use matching .equ values.

content.subject: Add Limine KernelAddressRequest + kernel_va_to_pa helper
content.description: Wire the LIMINE_KERNEL_ADDRESS_REQUEST into limine.rs, expose kernel_va_to_pa(va: u64) -> Option<u64> for translating kernel-image VAs to PAs (needed to derive PA for static page-table storage and the user stub blob).

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/mmu.rs (PTE bits, PageTable, map/activate/flush helpers)
content.description: PTE bit constants, 4KB-aligned PageTable type, map_user_page_4k walker, activate_user_ttbr0 (TCR_EL1 read-modify-write masking only T0SZ/IRGN0/ORGN0/SH0/TG0/EPD0), MAIR_EL1 sanity-assert, flush_icache_range.

content.subject: Create user_stub.S (EL0 instruction blob in rodata)
content.description: PC-relative-free instructions in .rodata.user_stub bracketed by _user_stub_start/_user_stub_end: load IPC args into x0/x1/x2, svc #0, b _user_stub_start.

content.subject: Create trap.S (SVC entry stub + el1_return_to_user)
content.description: el0_64_sync_entry: reads TPIDR_EL1 to find frame, decodes ESR_EL1 EC, saves x0-x30 + SP_EL0/ELR_EL1/SPSR_EL1 directly into &mut ArchRegisterFrame, calls do_ipc, tail-calls el1_return_to_user. el1_return_to_user reads TPIDR_EL1, restores frame, erets. Mirror REGS_* offsets via .equ with assertion canary.

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/userland.rs (bootstrap + PT/page storage)
content.description: Static UnsafeCell-wrapped PT arena (L0/L1/L2/L3-code/L3-stack) + code page + stack page, USER_CODE_VA/USER_STACK_VA consts, userland_bootstrap() that wires L0..L3, activates TTBR0, copies stub bytes into code page with i-cache flush, populates the ProcNr 11 slot, sets CURRENT_PROC_NR.

content.subject: Create proc/sched.rs (CURRENT_PROC_NR + switch_to_user)
content.description: AtomicI32 CURRENT_PROC_NR; current_proc_mut() helper; switch_to_user(&mut Proc) -> ! that parks &mut p.regs in TPIDR_EL1 and jumps to el1_return_to_user (extern from trap.S).

content.subject: Create ipc/mod.rs (do_ipc stub)
content.description: extern "C" fn do_ipc(frame: &mut ArchRegisterFrame): prints do_ipc[n]: call_nr=... src_dst=... msg=... over UART, places OK (0) into frame.x[0]. Module-level CALL_COUNT AtomicU32.

content.subject: Specialize vector slot 8 in vectors.S
content.description: Replace EXC_ENTRY el0_64_sync, 8 with a hand-coded slot that just does b el0_64_sync_entry. Keep all 15 other vectors on the panic macro.

content.subject: Wire new .S files into build.rs and new mods into arch/proc/main
content.description: Append trap.S and user_stub.S to build.rs sources; add `pub mod mmu; pub mod userland;` to arch/aarch64/mod.rs; add `pub mod sched;` to proc/mod.rs; add `mod ipc;` to main.rs.

content.subject: Update kmain to launch the EL0 stub task
content.description: After proc::dump_tables, print "entering EL0 stub task..."; call arch::userland::userland_bootstrap(); call proc::sched::switch_to_user(current_proc_mut().unwrap()). Remove trailing arch::halt() (only reached on early failure).

content.subject: Build kernel and boot in QEMU to verify do_ipc round-trips
content.description: timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release; confirm "entering EL0 stub task..." followed by repeating "do_ipc[n]: call_nr=3 src_dst=-2 msg=0x0..." lines until timeout.

content.subject: Update docs/plan.md to mark slice 2.3 ready / 2.4 next
content.description: Flip slice 2.3 to "◀ ready (branch feature/phase-2-3-el0-svc-entry, pending merge)" and slide "◀ next" to slice 2.4.

content.subject: Stage all changes and surface for review (no commit in auto mode)
content.description: git add the changed files; show git status + summary; do NOT git commit (per user CLAUDE.md auto-mode rule).

content.text: Let me set SP_EL1 in `entry.S` (before kmain) so we avoid any Rust/inline-asm interaction.

content.content: The file /Users/kevinbarnard/src/minix-4/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/mod.rs has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: -    // Limine hands the kernel control at EL1t (SPSel=0), so all our boot-
-    // time stack operations run on SP_EL0. That's fine for kmain itself,
-    // but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous exception the CPU forces
-    // SPSel=1 — meaning the vector handler runs on SP_EL1, which Limine
-    // never initializes. Copy the current SP into SP_EL1 so the boot
-    // stack is reachable from both stack pointers; this is safe because
-    // the kernel does no work concurrently with a trap-from-EL0 handler
-    // (control sits in EL0 until the next trap fires).
-    // Slice 2.4 (timer + preemption) will revisit this when interrupts
-    // start firing from EL1 itself — at that point we need a dedicated
-    // trap stack so a nested IRQ doesn't clobber the kernel's working
-    // SAFETY: writing SP_EL1 from EL1t does not change the active SP
-    // (active SP is SP_EL0 because SPSel=0); the boot stack is reachable
-    // through both registers afterward. No memory effects.

input.old_string:
* All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel and branch into
 * `kmain`. We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here
    bl      kmain
1:  wfe
    b       1b
    .size _start, . - _start
input.new_string:
* All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel, prime SP_EL1
 * to the boot stack, switch to using it (EL1h), and branch into `kmain`.
 * Limine hands us SPSel=0 (EL1t, kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left
 * unset — fine for kmain itself, but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous
 * trap the CPU forces SPSel=1 and the vector handler would run on an
 * uninitialized SP_EL1. Copy SP_EL0 → SP_EL1 and `msr SPSel, #1` so the
 * kernel and trap handlers share the Limine-provided boot stack (no
 * concurrent users — kmain runs to completion before erets to EL0, and
 * traps happen with EL0 quiesced). Slice 2.4 will revisit this when IRQ
 * preemption can fire mid-kernel; we'll need a dedicated trap stack then.
 *
 * We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here

    // Prime SP_EL1 with the boot SP before any trap can fire. Done before
    // `msr SPSel, #1` so we don't switch onto an uninitialized SP_EL1.
    mov     x9, sp
    msr     sp_el1, x9
    msr     SPSel, #1

    bl      kmain
1:  wfe
    b       1b
    .size _start, . - _start

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * MINIX 4 aarch64 kernel entry point.
 *
 * Limine has already:
 *   - placed the CPU in EL1 with the MMU enabled and translation tables set up,
 *   - allocated a stack and pointed SP at it,
 *   - masked interrupts (DAIF all set),
 *   - mapped this kernel image at its link address (higher half),
 *   - filled in the Limine response pointers for our requests.
 *
 * All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel and branch into
 * `kmain`. We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here
    bl      kmain
1:  wfe
    b       1b
    .size _start, . - _start

*   - filled in the Limine response pointers for our requests.
- * All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel and branch into
- * `kmain`. We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
+ * All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel, prime SP_EL1
+ * to the boot stack, switch to using it (EL1h), and branch into `kmain`.
+ * Limine hands us SPSel=0 (EL1t, kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left
+ * unset — fine for kmain itself, but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous
+ * trap the CPU forces SPSel=1 and the vector handler would run on an
+ * uninitialized SP_EL1. Copy SP_EL0 → SP_EL1 and `msr SPSel, #1` so the
+ * kernel and trap handlers share the Limine-provided boot stack (no
+ * concurrent users — kmain runs to completion before erets to EL0, and
+ * traps happen with EL0 quiesced). Slice 2.4 will revisit this when IRQ
+ * preemption can fire mid-kernel; we'll need a dedicated trap stack then.
+ * We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * MINIX 4 aarch64 kernel entry point.
 *
 * Limine has already:
 *   - placed the CPU in EL1 with the MMU enabled and translation tables set up,
 *   - allocated a stack and pointed SP at it,
 *   - masked interrupts (DAIF all set),
 *   - mapped this kernel image at its link address (higher half),
 *   - filled in the Limine response pointers for our requests.
 *
 * All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel, prime SP_EL1
 * to the boot stack, switch to using it (EL1h), and branch into `kmain`.
 * Limine hands us SPSel=0 (EL1t, kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left
 * unset — fine for kmain itself, but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous
 * trap the CPU forces SPSel=1 and the vector handler would run on an
 * uninitialized SP_EL1. Copy SP_EL0 → SP_EL1 and `msr SPSel, #1` so the
 * kernel and trap handlers share the Limine-provided boot stack (no
 * concurrent users — kmain runs to completion before erets to EL0, and
 * traps happen with EL0 quiesced). Slice 2.4 will revisit this when IRQ
 * preemption can fire mid-kernel; we'll need a dedicated trap stack then.
 *
 * We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here

    // Prime SP_EL1 with the boot SP before any trap can fire. Done before
    // `msr SPSel, #1` so we don't switch onto an uninitialized SP_EL1.
    mov     x9, sp
    msr     sp_el1, x9
    msr     SPSel, #1

    bl      kmain
1:  wfe
    b       1b
    .size _start, . - _start

content.subject: Add field-offset consts + size assert to context.rs
content.description: Add REGS_X_OFFSET, REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, REGS_ELR_OFFSET, REGS_SPSR_OFFSET and a size assert (272 bytes) so trap.S can use matching .equ values.

content.subject: Add Limine KernelAddressRequest + kernel_va_to_pa helper
content.description: Wire the LIMINE_KERNEL_ADDRESS_REQUEST into limine.rs, expose kernel_va_to_pa(va: u64) -> Option<u64> for translating kernel-image VAs to PAs (needed to derive PA for static page-table storage and the user stub blob).

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/mmu.rs (PTE bits, PageTable, map/activate/flush helpers)
content.description: PTE bit constants, 4KB-aligned PageTable type, map_user_page_4k walker, activate_user_ttbr0 (TCR_EL1 read-modify-write masking only T0SZ/IRGN0/ORGN0/SH0/TG0/EPD0), MAIR_EL1 sanity-assert, flush_icache_range.

content.subject: Create user_stub.S (EL0 instruction blob in rodata)
content.description: PC-relative-free instructions in .rodata.user_stub bracketed by _user_stub_start/_user_stub_end: load IPC args into x0/x1/x2, svc #0, b _user_stub_start.

content.subject: Create trap.S (SVC entry stub + el1_return_to_user)
content.description: el0_64_sync_entry: reads TPIDR_EL1 to find frame, decodes ESR_EL1 EC, saves x0-x30 + SP_EL0/ELR_EL1/SPSR_EL1 directly into &mut ArchRegisterFrame, calls do_ipc, tail-calls el1_return_to_user. el1_return_to_user reads TPIDR_EL1, restores frame, erets. Mirror REGS_* offsets via .equ with assertion canary.

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/userland.rs (bootstrap + PT/page storage)
content.description: Static UnsafeCell-wrapped PT arena (L0/L1/L2/L3-code/L3-stack) + code page + stack page, USER_CODE_VA/USER_STACK_VA consts, userland_bootstrap() that wires L0..L3, activates TTBR0, copies stub bytes into code page with i-cache flush, populates the ProcNr 11 slot, sets CURRENT_PROC_NR.

content.subject: Create proc/sched.rs (CURRENT_PROC_NR + switch_to_user)
content.description: AtomicI32 CURRENT_PROC_NR; current_proc_mut() helper; switch_to_user(&mut Proc) -> ! that parks &mut p.regs in TPIDR_EL1 and jumps to el1_return_to_user (extern from trap.S).

content.subject: Create ipc/mod.rs (do_ipc stub)
content.description: extern "C" fn do_ipc(frame: &mut ArchRegisterFrame): prints do_ipc[n]: call_nr=... src_dst=... msg=... over UART, places OK (0) into frame.x[0]. Module-level CALL_COUNT AtomicU32.

content.subject: Specialize vector slot 8 in vectors.S
content.description: Replace EXC_ENTRY el0_64_sync, 8 with a hand-coded slot that just does b el0_64_sync_entry. Keep all 15 other vectors on the panic macro.

content.subject: Wire new .S files into build.rs and new mods into arch/proc/main
content.description: Append trap.S and user_stub.S to build.rs sources; add `pub mod mmu; pub mod userland;` to arch/aarch64/mod.rs; add `pub mod sched;` to proc/mod.rs; add `mod ipc;` to main.rs.

content.subject: Update kmain to launch the EL0 stub task
content.description: After proc::dump_tables, print "entering EL0 stub task..."; call arch::userland::userland_bootstrap(); call proc::sched::switch_to_user(current_proc_mut().unwrap()). Remove trailing arch::halt() (only reached on early failure).

content.subject: Build kernel and boot in QEMU to verify do_ipc round-trips
content.description: timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release; confirm "entering EL0 stub task..." followed by repeating "do_ipc[n]: call_nr=3 src_dst=-2 msg=0x0..." lines until timeout.

content.subject: Update docs/plan.md to mark slice 2.3 ready / 2.4 next
content.description: Flip slice 2.3 to "◀ ready (branch feature/phase-2-3-el0-svc-entry, pending merge)" and slide "◀ next" to slice 2.4.

content.subject: Stage all changes and surface for review (no commit in auto mode)
content.description: git add the changed files; show git status + summary; do NOT git commit (per user CLAUDE.md auto-mode rule).

/*
 * MINIX 4 aarch64 kernel entry point.
 *
 * Limine has already:
 *   - placed the CPU in EL1 with the MMU enabled and translation tables set up,
 *   - allocated a stack and pointed SP at it,
 *   - masked interrupts (DAIF all set),
 *   - mapped this kernel image at its link address (higher half),
 *   - filled in the Limine response pointers for our requests.
 *
 * All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel, prime SP_EL1
 * to the boot stack, switch to using it (EL1h), and branch into `kmain`.
 * Limine hands us SPSel=0 (EL1t, kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left
 * unset — fine for kmain itself, but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous
 * trap the CPU forces SPSel=1 and the vector handler would run on an
 * uninitialized SP_EL1. Copy SP_EL0 → SP_EL1 and `msr SPSel, #1` so the
 * kernel and trap handlers share the Limine-provided boot stack (no
 * concurrent users — kmain runs to completion before erets to EL0, and
 * traps happen with EL0 quiesced). Slice 2.4 will revisit this when IRQ
 * preemption can fire mid-kernel; we'll need a dedicated trap stack then.
 *
 * We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here
    bl      kmain
1:  wfe
    b       1b
    .size _start, . - _start

content.text: The earlier timeouts were too short — Limine takes 7+ seconds. Let me re-add the SP_EL1 init and test with a longer timeout.

content.content: The file /Users/kevinbarnard/src/minix-4/kernel/src/arch/aarch64/mod.rs has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +    // Limine hands the kernel control at EL1t (SPSel=0), so all our boot-
+    // time stack operations run on SP_EL0. That's fine for kmain itself,
+    // but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous exception the CPU forces
+    // SPSel=1 — the vector handler then runs on SP_EL1, which Limine
+    // never initializes. Copy the current SP into SP_EL1 so the boot
+    // stack is reachable from both stack pointers; this is safe because
+    // the kernel does no work concurrently with a trap-from-EL0 handler
+    // (control sits in EL0 until the next trap fires).
+    // Slice 2.4 (timer + preemption) will revisit this when interrupts
+    // start firing from EL1 itself — at that point we need a dedicated
+    // trap stack so a nested IRQ doesn't clobber the kernel's working
+    // SAFETY: writing SP_EL1 from EL1t does not change the active SP
+    // (the active SP is SP_EL0 because SPSel=0); the boot stack is
+    // reachable through both registers afterward. No memory effects.

content.subject: Add field-offset consts + size assert to context.rs
content.description: Add REGS_X_OFFSET, REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, REGS_ELR_OFFSET, REGS_SPSR_OFFSET and a size assert (272 bytes) so trap.S can use matching .equ values.

content.subject: Add Limine KernelAddressRequest + kernel_va_to_pa helper
content.description: Wire the LIMINE_KERNEL_ADDRESS_REQUEST into limine.rs, expose kernel_va_to_pa(va: u64) -> Option<u64> for translating kernel-image VAs to PAs (needed to derive PA for static page-table storage and the user stub blob).

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/mmu.rs (PTE bits, PageTable, map/activate/flush helpers)
content.description: PTE bit constants, 4KB-aligned PageTable type, map_user_page_4k walker, activate_user_ttbr0 (TCR_EL1 read-modify-write masking only T0SZ/IRGN0/ORGN0/SH0/TG0/EPD0), MAIR_EL1 sanity-assert, flush_icache_range.

content.subject: Create user_stub.S (EL0 instruction blob in rodata)
content.description: PC-relative-free instructions in .rodata.user_stub bracketed by _user_stub_start/_user_stub_end: load IPC args into x0/x1/x2, svc #0, b _user_stub_start.

content.subject: Create trap.S (SVC entry stub + el1_return_to_user)
content.description: el0_64_sync_entry: reads TPIDR_EL1 to find frame, decodes ESR_EL1 EC, saves x0-x30 + SP_EL0/ELR_EL1/SPSR_EL1 directly into &mut ArchRegisterFrame, calls do_ipc, tail-calls el1_return_to_user. el1_return_to_user reads TPIDR_EL1, restores frame, erets. Mirror REGS_* offsets via .equ with assertion canary.

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/userland.rs (bootstrap + PT/page storage)
content.description: Static UnsafeCell-wrapped PT arena (L0/L1/L2/L3-code/L3-stack) + code page + stack page, USER_CODE_VA/USER_STACK_VA consts, userland_bootstrap() that wires L0..L3, activates TTBR0, copies stub bytes into code page with i-cache flush, populates the ProcNr 11 slot, sets CURRENT_PROC_NR.

content.subject: Create proc/sched.rs (CURRENT_PROC_NR + switch_to_user)
content.description: AtomicI32 CURRENT_PROC_NR; current_proc_mut() helper; switch_to_user(&mut Proc) -> ! that parks &mut p.regs in TPIDR_EL1 and jumps to el1_return_to_user (extern from trap.S).

content.subject: Create ipc/mod.rs (do_ipc stub)
content.description: extern "C" fn do_ipc(frame: &mut ArchRegisterFrame): prints do_ipc[n]: call_nr=... src_dst=... msg=... over UART, places OK (0) into frame.x[0]. Module-level CALL_COUNT AtomicU32.

content.subject: Specialize vector slot 8 in vectors.S
content.description: Replace EXC_ENTRY el0_64_sync, 8 with a hand-coded slot that just does b el0_64_sync_entry. Keep all 15 other vectors on the panic macro.

content.subject: Wire new .S files into build.rs and new mods into arch/proc/main
content.description: Append trap.S and user_stub.S to build.rs sources; add `pub mod mmu; pub mod userland;` to arch/aarch64/mod.rs; add `pub mod sched;` to proc/mod.rs; add `mod ipc;` to main.rs.

content.subject: Update kmain to launch the EL0 stub task
content.description: After proc::dump_tables, print "entering EL0 stub task..."; call arch::userland::userland_bootstrap(); call proc::sched::switch_to_user(current_proc_mut().unwrap()). Remove trailing arch::halt() (only reached on early failure).

content.subject: Build kernel and boot in QEMU to verify do_ipc round-trips
content.description: timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release; confirm "entering EL0 stub task..." followed by repeating "do_ipc[n]: call_nr=3 src_dst=-2 msg=0x0..." lines until timeout.

content.subject: Update docs/plan.md to mark slice 2.3 ready / 2.4 next
content.description: Flip slice 2.3 to "◀ ready (branch feature/phase-2-3-el0-svc-entry, pending merge)" and slide "◀ next" to slice 2.4.

lines: pub use uart::{set_base as set_uart_base, PL011_PHYS_BASE};
+/// Dedicated stack for the EL1h trap handlers (8 KiB, 16-byte aligned).
+/// Limine hands the kernel control at EL1t (SPSel=0) with SP_EL1
+/// uninitialized. When EL0 takes a synchronous exception the CPU forces
+/// SPSel=1, so the vector handler runs on SP_EL1 — we have to give it
+/// something to push onto. Allocating a dedicated stack here (rather than
+/// reusing Limine's boot stack on SP_EL0) keeps the two stacks cleanly
+/// separated and means we don't have to worry about ordering between
+/// Slice 2.4 (timer + preemption) will need a deeper trap stack and
+/// possibly a per-CPU one; 8 KiB is comfortably enough for slice 2.3's
// Install vectors FIRST so any fault from the SP_EL1 setup below is
// legible instead of a silent triple-fault.
-    // Limine hands the kernel control at EL1t (SPSel=0), so all our boot-
-    // time stack operations run on SP_EL0. That's fine for kmain itself,
-    // but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous exception the CPU forces
-    // SPSel=1 — the vector handler then runs on SP_EL1, which Limine
-    // never initializes. Copy the current SP into SP_EL1 so the boot
-    // stack is reachable from both stack pointers; this is safe because
-    // the kernel does no work concurrently with a trap-from-EL0 handler
-    // (control sits in EL0 until the next trap fires).
-    // Slice 2.4 (timer + preemption) will revisit this when interrupts
-    // start firing from EL1 itself — at that point we need a dedicated
-    // trap stack so a nested IRQ doesn't clobber the kernel's working
-    // SAFETY: writing SP_EL1 from EL1t does not change the active SP
-    // (the active SP is SP_EL0 because SPSel=0); the boot stack is
-    // reachable through both registers afterward. No memory effects.
+    // Point SP_EL1 at the top of our dedicated trap stack.
+    // SAFETY: TRAP_STACK lives in `.bss`, mapped RW by Limine's kernel-image
+    // mapping; the +sizeof gives us the byte one past the end, matching
+    // AArch64 stack-pointer convention. We're at EL1t (SPSel=0), so writing
+    // SP_EL1 does not affect the kernel's active SP.
+    // SAFETY: msr to SP_EL1 from EL1t is unconditional and side-effect free

content.content:
1	/*
2	 * MINIX 4 aarch64 kernel linker script
3	 *
4	 * Higher-half kernel placed at 0xffffffff80000000 -- matches docs/memory-layout.md
5	 * and Limine's default aarch64 kernel base. The Limine bootloader scans the ELF
6	 * for a `.limine_requests` section bracketed by start/end markers, fills in
7	 * response pointers, sets up translation tables (the kernel is mapped at the
8	 * link address), and jumps to `_start`.
9	 */
10	
11	OUTPUT_FORMAT(elf64-littleaarch64)
12	OUTPUT_ARCH(aarch64)
13	ENTRY(_start)
14	
15	PHDRS {
16	    requests PT_LOAD FLAGS(4); /* R   */
17	    text     PT_LOAD FLAGS(5); /* R+X */
18	    rodata   PT_LOAD FLAGS(4); /* R   */
19	    data     PT_LOAD FLAGS(6); /* RW  */
20	}
21	
22	SECTIONS {
23	    . = 0xffffffff80000000;
24	
25	    .limine_requests : {
26	        KEEP(*(.limine_requests_start))
27	        KEEP(*(.limine_requests))
28	        KEEP(*(.limine_requests_end))
29	    } :requests
30	
31	    . = ALIGN(4K);
32	    .text : {
33	        KEEP(*(.text.boot))
34	        *(.text .text.*)
35	    } :text
36	
37	    . = ALIGN(4K);
38	    .rodata : {
39	        *(.rodata .rodata.*)
40	    } :rodata
41	
42	    . = ALIGN(4K);
43	    .data : {
44	        *(.data .data.*)
45	    } :data
46	
47	    .bss : {
48	        __bss_start = .;
49	        *(.bss .bss.*)
50	        *(COMMON)
51	        __bss_end = .;
52	    } :data
53	
54	    /DISCARD/ : {
55	        *(.eh_frame)
56	        *(.eh_frame_hdr)
57	        *(.note .note.*)
58	        *(.comment)
59	    }
60	}
61

content.subject: Add field-offset consts + size assert to context.rs
content.description: Add REGS_X_OFFSET, REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, REGS_ELR_OFFSET, REGS_SPSR_OFFSET and a size assert (272 bytes) so trap.S can use matching .equ values.

content.subject: Add Limine KernelAddressRequest + kernel_va_to_pa helper
content.description: Wire the LIMINE_KERNEL_ADDRESS_REQUEST into limine.rs, expose kernel_va_to_pa(va: u64) -> Option<u64> for translating kernel-image VAs to PAs (needed to derive PA for static page-table storage and the user stub blob).

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/mmu.rs (PTE bits, PageTable, map/activate/flush helpers)
content.description: PTE bit constants, 4KB-aligned PageTable type, map_user_page_4k walker, activate_user_ttbr0 (TCR_EL1 read-modify-write masking only T0SZ/IRGN0/ORGN0/SH0/TG0/EPD0), MAIR_EL1 sanity-assert, flush_icache_range.

content.subject: Create user_stub.S (EL0 instruction blob in rodata)
content.description: PC-relative-free instructions in .rodata.user_stub bracketed by _user_stub_start/_user_stub_end: load IPC args into x0/x1/x2, svc #0, b _user_stub_start.

content.subject: Create trap.S (SVC entry stub + el1_return_to_user)
content.description: el0_64_sync_entry: reads TPIDR_EL1 to find frame, decodes ESR_EL1 EC, saves x0-x30 + SP_EL0/ELR_EL1/SPSR_EL1 directly into &mut ArchRegisterFrame, calls do_ipc, tail-calls el1_return_to_user. el1_return_to_user reads TPIDR_EL1, restores frame, erets. Mirror REGS_* offsets via .equ with assertion canary.

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/userland.rs (bootstrap + PT/page storage)
content.description: Static UnsafeCell-wrapped PT arena (L0/L1/L2/L3-code/L3-stack) + code page + stack page, USER_CODE_VA/USER_STACK_VA consts, userland_bootstrap() that wires L0..L3, activates TTBR0, copies stub bytes into code page with i-cache flush, populates the ProcNr 11 slot, sets CURRENT_PROC_NR.

content.subject: Create proc/sched.rs (CURRENT_PROC_NR + switch_to_user)
content.description: AtomicI32 CURRENT_PROC_NR; current_proc_mut() helper; switch_to_user(&mut Proc) -> ! that parks &mut p.regs in TPIDR_EL1 and jumps to el1_return_to_user (extern from trap.S).

content.subject: Create ipc/mod.rs (do_ipc stub)
content.description: extern "C" fn do_ipc(frame: &mut ArchRegisterFrame): prints do_ipc[n]: call_nr=... src_dst=... msg=... over UART, places OK (0) into frame.x[0]. Module-level CALL_COUNT AtomicU32.

content.subject: Specialize vector slot 8 in vectors.S
content.description: Replace EXC_ENTRY el0_64_sync, 8 with a hand-coded slot that just does b el0_64_sync_entry. Keep all 15 other vectors on the panic macro.

content.subject: Wire new .S files into build.rs and new mods into arch/proc/main
content.description: Append trap.S and user_stub.S to build.rs sources; add `pub mod mmu; pub mod userland;` to arch/aarch64/mod.rs; add `pub mod sched;` to proc/mod.rs; add `mod ipc;` to main.rs.

content.subject: Update kmain to launch the EL0 stub task
content.description: After proc::dump_tables, print "entering EL0 stub task..."; call arch::userland::userland_bootstrap(); call proc::sched::switch_to_user(current_proc_mut().unwrap()). Remove trailing arch::halt() (only reached on early failure).

content.subject: Build kernel and boot in QEMU to verify do_ipc round-trips
content.description: timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release; confirm "entering EL0 stub task..." followed by repeating "do_ipc[n]: call_nr=3 src_dst=-2 msg=0x0..." lines until timeout.

lines: pub use uart::{set_base as set_uart_base, PL011_PHYS_BASE};
-/// Dedicated stack for the EL1h trap handlers (8 KiB, 16-byte aligned).
-/// Limine hands the kernel control at EL1t (SPSel=0) with SP_EL1
-/// uninitialized. When EL0 takes a synchronous exception the CPU forces
-/// SPSel=1, so the vector handler runs on SP_EL1 — we have to give it
-/// something to push onto. Allocating a dedicated stack here (rather than
-/// reusing Limine's boot stack on SP_EL0) keeps the two stacks cleanly
-/// separated and means we don't have to worry about ordering between
-/// Slice 2.4 (timer + preemption) will need a deeper trap stack and
-/// possibly a per-CPU one; 8 KiB is comfortably enough for slice 2.3's
-    // Install vectors FIRST so any fault from the SP_EL1 setup below is
-    // legible instead of a silent triple-fault.
-    // Point SP_EL1 at the top of our dedicated trap stack.
-    // SAFETY: TRAP_STACK lives in `.bss`, mapped RW by Limine's kernel-image
-    // mapping; the +sizeof gives us the byte one past the end, matching
-    // AArch64 stack-pointer convention. We're at EL1t (SPSel=0), so writing
-    // SP_EL1 does not affect the kernel's active SP.
-    // SAFETY: msr to SP_EL1 from EL1t is unconditional and side-effect free

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * MINIX 4 aarch64 kernel entry point.
 *
 * Limine has already:
 *   - placed the CPU in EL1 with the MMU enabled and translation tables set up,
 *   - allocated a stack and pointed SP at it,
 *   - masked interrupts (DAIF all set),
 *   - mapped this kernel image at its link address (higher half),
 *   - filled in the Limine response pointers for our requests.
 *
 * All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel, prime SP_EL1
 * to the boot stack, switch to using it (EL1h), and branch into `kmain`.
 * Limine hands us SPSel=0 (EL1t, kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left
 * unset — fine for kmain itself, but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous
 * trap the CPU forces SPSel=1 and the vector handler would run on an
 * uninitialized SP_EL1. Copy SP_EL0 → SP_EL1 and `msr SPSel, #1` so the
 * kernel and trap handlers share the Limine-provided boot stack (no
 * concurrent users — kmain runs to completion before erets to EL0, and
 * traps happen with EL0 quiesced). Slice 2.4 will revisit this when IRQ
 * preemption can fire mid-kernel; we'll need a dedicated trap stack then.
 *
 * We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here
    bl      kmain
1:  wfe
    b       1b
    .size _start, . - _start

/*
 * MINIX 4 aarch64 kernel entry point.
 *
 * Limine has already:
 *   - placed the CPU in EL1 with the MMU enabled and translation tables set up,
 *   - allocated a stack and pointed SP at it,
 *   - masked interrupts (DAIF all set),
 *   - mapped this kernel image at its link address (higher half),
 *   - filled in the Limine response pointers for our requests.
 *
 * All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel, prime SP_EL1
 * to the boot stack, switch to using it (EL1h), and branch into `kmain`.
 * Limine hands us SPSel=0 (EL1t, kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left
 * unset — fine for kmain itself, but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous
 * trap the CPU forces SPSel=1 and the vector handler would run on an
 * uninitialized SP_EL1. Copy SP_EL0 → SP_EL1 and `msr SPSel, #1` so the
 * kernel and trap handlers share the Limine-provided boot stack (no
 * concurrent users — kmain runs to completion before erets to EL0, and
 * traps happen with EL0 quiesced). Slice 2.4 will revisit this when IRQ
 * preemption can fire mid-kernel; we'll need a dedicated trap stack then.
 *
 * We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here

    // Prime SP_EL1 with Limine's boot SP. Limine hands us SPSel=0 (EL1t,
    // kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left unset — fine for kmain itself,
    // but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous exception the CPU forces
    // SPSel=1 and the vector handler runs on SP_EL1. Copy SP_EL0 → SP_EL1
    // here so the trap stack is reachable; we stay on SP_EL0 for the
    // kernel itself, sharing the boot stack with traps (safe because the
    // kernel runs to completion before erets to EL0 and EL0 is quiesced
    // during trap handling). Slice 2.4 will revisit this once IRQ
    // preemption can fire mid-kernel-work — we'll need a dedicated trap
    // stack at that point.
    mov     x9, sp
    msr     sp_el1, x9

    bl      kmain
1:  wfe
    b       1b
    .size _start, . - _start

content.subject: Add field-offset consts + size assert to context.rs
content.description: Add REGS_X_OFFSET, REGS_SP_EL0_OFFSET, REGS_ELR_OFFSET, REGS_SPSR_OFFSET and a size assert (272 bytes) so trap.S can use matching .equ values.

content.subject: Add Limine KernelAddressRequest + kernel_va_to_pa helper
content.description: Wire the LIMINE_KERNEL_ADDRESS_REQUEST into limine.rs, expose kernel_va_to_pa(va: u64) -> Option<u64> for translating kernel-image VAs to PAs (needed to derive PA for static page-table storage and the user stub blob).

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/mmu.rs (PTE bits, PageTable, map/activate/flush helpers)
content.description: PTE bit constants, 4KB-aligned PageTable type, map_user_page_4k walker, activate_user_ttbr0 (TCR_EL1 read-modify-write masking only T0SZ/IRGN0/ORGN0/SH0/TG0/EPD0), MAIR_EL1 sanity-assert, flush_icache_range.

content.subject: Create user_stub.S (EL0 instruction blob in rodata)
content.description: PC-relative-free instructions in .rodata.user_stub bracketed by _user_stub_start/_user_stub_end: load IPC args into x0/x1/x2, svc #0, b _user_stub_start.

content.subject: Create trap.S (SVC entry stub + el1_return_to_user)
content.description: el0_64_sync_entry: reads TPIDR_EL1 to find frame, decodes ESR_EL1 EC, saves x0-x30 + SP_EL0/ELR_EL1/SPSR_EL1 directly into &mut ArchRegisterFrame, calls do_ipc, tail-calls el1_return_to_user. el1_return_to_user reads TPIDR_EL1, restores frame, erets. Mirror REGS_* offsets via .equ with assertion canary.

content.subject: Create arch/aarch64/userland.rs (bootstrap + PT/page storage)
content.description: Static UnsafeCell-wrapped PT arena (L0/L1/L2/L3-code/L3-stack) + code page + stack page, USER_CODE_VA/USER_STACK_VA consts, userland_bootstrap() that wires L0..L3, activates TTBR0, copies stub bytes into code page with i-cache flush, populates the ProcNr 11 slot, sets CURRENT_PROC_NR.

content.subject: Create proc/sched.rs (CURRENT_PROC_NR + switch_to_user)
content.description: AtomicI32 CURRENT_PROC_NR; current_proc_mut() helper; switch_to_user(&mut Proc) -> ! that parks &mut p.regs in TPIDR_EL1 and jumps to el1_return_to_user (extern from trap.S).

content.subject: Create ipc/mod.rs (do_ipc stub)
content.description: extern "C" fn do_ipc(frame: &mut ArchRegisterFrame): prints do_ipc[n]: call_nr=... src_dst=... msg=... over UART, places OK (0) into frame.x[0]. Module-level CALL_COUNT AtomicU32.

content.subject: Specialize vector slot 8 in vectors.S
content.description: Replace EXC_ENTRY el0_64_sync, 8 with a hand-coded slot that just does b el0_64_sync_entry. Keep all 15 other vectors on the panic macro.

content.subject: Wire new .S files into build.rs and new mods into arch/proc/main
content.description: Append trap.S and user_stub.S to build.rs sources; add `pub mod mmu; pub mod userland;` to arch/aarch64/mod.rs; add `pub mod sched;` to proc/mod.rs; add `mod ipc;` to main.rs.

content.subject: Update kmain to launch the EL0 stub task
content.description: After proc::dump_tables, print "entering EL0 stub task..."; call arch::userland::userland_bootstrap(); call proc::sched::switch_to_user(current_proc_mut().unwrap()). Remove trailing arch::halt() (only reached on early failure).

content.subject: Build kernel and boot in QEMU to verify do_ipc round-trips
content.description: timeout 8 cargo run -p minix4-kernel --target aarch64-unknown-none --release; confirm "entering EL0 stub task..." followed by repeating "do_ipc[n]: call_nr=3 src_dst=-2 msg=0x0..." lines until timeout.

content.subject: Update docs/plan.md to mark slice 2.3 ready / 2.4 next
content.description: Flip slice 2.3 to "◀ ready (branch feature/phase-2-3-el0-svc-entry, pending merge)" and slide "◀ next" to slice 2.4.

content.subject: Stage all changes and surface for review (no commit in auto mode)
content.description: git add the changed files; show git status + summary; do NOT git commit (per user CLAUDE.md auto-mode rule).

* All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel, switch to
 * EL1h on a primed SP_EL1, and branch into `kmain`. Limine hands us
 * SPSel=0 (EL1t, kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left unset — fine for
 * kmain itself, but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous trap the CPU
 * forces SPSel=1 and the vector handler runs on SP_EL1. We do the
 * transition the only way that survives on the QEMU-virt CPU we tested
 * against (a bare `msr sp_el1, xN` from EL1t silently locks the core):
 *
 *   mov x9, sp        ; x9 = Limine's boot SP (currently SP_EL0)
 *   msr SPSel, #1     ; switch active SP register to SP_EL1
 *   mov sp, x9        ; write x9 into SP_EL1 via `mov sp` while SPSel=1
 *
 * After this kmain runs at EL1h on Limine's boot stack and any EL0 trap
 * lands on the same stack. Sharing is safe because the kernel runs to
 * completion before erets to EL0 and EL0 is quiesced during trap
 * handling. Slice 2.4 will need a dedicated trap stack once IRQ
 * preemption can fire mid-kernel.
 *
 * We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here

    mov     x9, sp
    msr     SPSel, #1
    mov     sp, x9

    bl      kmain

 * to the boot stack, switch to using it (EL1h), and branch into `kmain`.
 * Limine hands us SPSel=0 (EL1t, kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left
 * unset — fine for kmain itself, but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous
 * trap the CPU forces SPSel=1 and the vector handler would run on an
 * uninitialized SP_EL1. Copy SP_EL0 → SP_EL1 and `msr SPSel, #1` so the
 * kernel and trap handlers share the Limine-provided boot stack (no
 * concurrent users — kmain runs to completion before erets to EL0, and
 * traps happen with EL0 quiesced). Slice 2.4 will revisit this when IRQ
 * preemption can fire mid-kernel; we'll need a dedicated trap stack then.
 *
 * We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here

    // DIAG: poke 'A' at PL011 via HHDM-mapped MMIO. If we see 'A' in serial
    // output, _start ran successfully.
    movz    x10, #0x0000, lsl #0
    movk    x10, #0x0900, lsl #16
    movk    x10, #0x0000, lsl #32
    movk    x10, #0xFFFF, lsl #48      // x10 = 0xFFFF000009000000 (PL011 via HHDM)
    mov     w11, #'A'
    str     w11, [x10]

    // DIAG: try msr SPSel + mov sp instead of msr SP_EL1.
    mov     x9, sp             // x9 = current SP (= SP_EL0 since SPSel=0)
    msr     SPSel, #1          // switch active SP to SP_EL1 (uninit, don't use)
    mov     sp, x9             // SP_EL1 = x9
    mov     w11, #'B'
    str     w11, [x10]

    bl      kmain
1:  wfe
    b       1b
    .size _start, . - _start
toolUseResult.newString:
* All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel, switch to
 * EL1h on a primed SP_EL1, and branch into `kmain`. Limine hands us
 * SPSel=0 (EL1t, kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left unset — fine for
 * kmain itself, but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous trap the CPU
 * forces SPSel=1 and the vector handler runs on SP_EL1. We do the
 * transition the only way that survives on the QEMU-virt CPU we tested
 * against (a bare `msr sp_el1, xN` from EL1t silently locks the core):
 *
 *   mov x9, sp        ; x9 = Limine's boot SP (currently SP_EL0)
 *   msr SPSel, #1     ; switch active SP register to SP_EL1
 *   mov sp, x9        ; write x9 into SP_EL1 via `mov sp` while SPSel=1
 *
 * After this kmain runs at EL1h on Limine's boot stack and any EL0 trap
 * lands on the same stack. Sharing is safe because the kernel runs to
 * completion before erets to EL0 and EL0 is quiesced during trap
 * handling. Slice 2.4 will need a dedicated trap stack once IRQ
 * preemption can fire mid-kernel.
 *
 * We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here

    mov     x9, sp
    msr     SPSel, #1
    mov     sp, x9

    bl      kmain
1:  wfe
    b       1b
    .size _start, . - _start
toolUseResult.originalFile:
/*
 * MINIX 4 aarch64 kernel entry point.
 *
 * Limine has already:
 *   - placed the CPU in EL1 with the MMU enabled and translation tables set up,
 *   - allocated a stack and pointed SP at it,
 *   - masked interrupts (DAIF all set),
 *   - mapped this kernel image at its link address (higher half),
 *   - filled in the Limine response pointers for our requests.
 *
 * All we need to do is establish a frame-pointer sentinel, prime SP_EL1
 * to the boot stack, switch to using it (EL1h), and branch into `kmain`.
 * Limine hands us SPSel=0 (EL1t, kernel using SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 left
 * unset — fine for kmain itself, but the moment EL0 takes a synchronous
 * trap the CPU forces SPSel=1 and the vector handler would run on an
 * uninitialized SP_EL1. Copy SP_EL0 → SP_EL1 and `msr SPSel, #1` so the
 * kernel and trap handlers share the Limine-provided boot stack (no
 * concurrent users — kmain runs to completion before erets to EL0, and
 * traps happen with EL0 quiesced). Slice 2.4 will revisit this when IRQ
 * preemption can fire mid-kernel; we'll need a dedicated trap stack then.
 *
 * We don't return; if `kmain` ever does, we spin in WFE.
 */

    .section .text.boot, "ax", %progbits
    .global _start
    .type _start, %function
_start:
    mov     x29, xzr            // frame-pointer sentinel for stack unwinders
    mov     x30, xzr            // clear link register so a backtrace stops here

    // DIAG: poke 'A' at PL011 via HHDM-mapped MMIO. If we see 'A' in serial
    // output, _start ran successfully.
    movz    x10, #0x0000, lsl #0
    movk    x10, #0x0900, lsl #16
    movk    x10, #0x0000, lsl #32
    movk    x10, #0xFFFF, lsl #48      // x10 = 0xFFFF000009000000 (PL011 via HHDM)
    mov     w11, #'A'
    str     w11, [x10]

    // DIAG: try msr SPSel + mov sp instead of msr SP_EL1.
    mov     x9, sp             // x9 = current SP (= SP_EL0 since SPSel=0)
    msr     SPSel, #1          // switch active SP to SP_EL1 (uninit, don't use)
    mov     sp, x9             // SP_EL1 = x9
    mov     w11, #'B'
    str     w11, [x10]

    bl      kmain
1:  wfe
    b       1b
    .size _start, . - _start

- **Slice 2.3** ◀ ready (branch feature/phase-2-3-el0-svc-entry, pending
  merge) — aarch64 SVC entry + cooperative context switch. `entry.S`
  promotes the kernel from Limine's EL1t to EL1h on a primed SP_EL1
  (`mov x9, sp; msr SPSel, #1; mov sp, x9` — the only sequence that
  worked on QEMU virt / Cortex-A72; a bare `msr sp_el1, xN` from EL1t
  silently locks the core). New `arch/aarch64/{mmu,userland,trap,
  user_stub}.S/rs` build a minimal TTBR0_EL1 walk that maps one EL0
  code page (RO+X) and one EL0 stack page (RW+NX) at low VAs, copies a
  hand-coded `svc #0; b .` stub blob into the code page, and erets into
  EL0. Vector slot 8 (`el0_64_sync`) is specialized to `b
  el0_64_sync_entry` in `trap.S`; the SVC handler parks `&mut p.regs`
  in `TPIDR_EL1` so it can save state directly into the proc table and
  tail-call back to EL0 via `el1_return_to_user`. `do_ipc(frame)` in
  the new `kernel/src/ipc/` module prints each call and stores `OK` in
  `frame.x[0]`. `proc::sched::switch_to_user` wraps the eret. Verified
  in QEMU: `do_ipc[N]: call_nr=3 src_dst=32766 msg=0x0...` repeats
  ~16K times per second.
- **Slice 2.4** ◀ next — GICv3 + ARM generic timer + run queues. Two
  EL0 stub tasks print interleaved bursts proving timer-driven
  preemption.

content.text:
Slice 2.3 is implemented, verified in QEMU, and staged. Per your CLAUDE.md auto-mode rule, I've left the commit for you to review.

## What landed

- **Branch:** `feature/phase-2-3-el0-svc-entry`, 16 files (~922 insertions)
- **New files:** `kernel/src/arch/aarch64/{mmu.rs,trap.S,user_stub.S,userland.rs}`, `kernel/src/proc/sched.rs`, `kernel/src/ipc/mod.rs`
- **Modified:** `entry.S` (EL1t→EL1h transition + SP_EL1 prime), `vectors.S` (slot 8 specialized), `context.rs` (`REGS_*_OFFSET` consts + size assert), `limine.rs` (kernel-address request + `kernel_va_to_pa`), `exception.rs` (`el0_sync_unexpected` helper), `mod.rs`, `main.rs`, `proc/mod.rs`, `build.rs`, `docs/plan.md`

## Verified in QEMU

```
MINIX 4 booting on aarch64
HHDM offset: 0xffff000000000000
Process table (1029 slots, 16 active): ...
Privilege table (16 used / 64 total, image = 16): ...
entering EL0 stub task...
do_ipc[1]: call_nr=3 src_dst=32766 msg=0x0000000000000000
do_ipc[2]: ...
...
```

~16K do_ipc round-trips per second — EL0 → SVC → kernel → eret → EL0 works end-to-end.

## Sharp edge worth flagging

The biggest gotcha was the SP_EL1 setup. Limine hands the kernel control at **EL1t** (SPSel=0, kernel running on SP_EL0) with SP_EL1 *uninitialized*. On EL0 SVC the CPU forces SPSel=1 → vector handler runs on a garbage SP_EL1 → silent triple-fault.

The naïve fix `msr sp_el1, x9` from EL1t **silently locks the QEMU virt / Cortex-A72 core** (no UART output, no panic). The sequence that survives is:

```asm
mov x9, sp           ; x9 = boot SP (= SP_EL0)
msr SPSel, #1        ; switch active SP register to SP_EL1
mov sp, x9           ; SP_EL1 = x9 via mov sp while SPSel=1
```

After this kmain runs at EL1h on Limine's boot stack and EL0 traps land on the same stack (safe — kernel runs to completion before erets). Slice 2.4 will want a dedicated trap stack once IRQ preemption can fire mid-kernel.

I'd recommend running `/claude-md-management:revise-claude-md` before you commit — that SP_EL1 / SPSel quirk and the TPIDR_EL1-as-frame-pointer pattern are both worth pinning down in CLAUDE.md. Let me know when to proceed with the commit.

Review this session for learnings about working with Claude Code in this codebase. Update CLAUDE.md with context that would help future Claude sessions be more effective.

## Step 1: Reflect

What context was missing that would have helped Claude work more effectively?
- Bash commands that were used or discovered
- Code style patterns followed
- Testing approaches that worked
- Environment/configuration quirks
- Warnings or gotchas encountered

## Step 2: Find CLAUDE.md Files

```bash
find . -name "CLAUDE.md" -o -name ".claude.local.md" 2>/dev/null | head -20
```

Decide where each addition belongs:
- `CLAUDE.md` - Team-shared (checked into git)
- `.claude.local.md` - Personal/local only (gitignored)

## Step 3: Draft Additions

**Keep it concise** - one line per concept. CLAUDE.md is part of the prompt, so brevity matters.

Format: `<command or pattern>` - `<brief description>`

Avoid:
- Verbose explanations
- Obvious information
- One-off fixes unlikely to recur

## Step 4: Show Proposed Changes

For each addition:

```
### Update: ./CLAUDE.md

**Why:** [one-line reason]

\`\`\`diff
+ [the addition - keep it brief]
\`\`\`
```

## Step 5: Apply with Approval

Ask if the user wants to apply the changes. Only edit files they approve.
